title: Monitoring & Alerting Basics
description:
## Knowing What Your System Is Doing

Once software is running, you need to know whether it's healthy. That's the job of **monitoring**.

### Monitoring vs observability

| Term | What it means |
|---|---|
| **Monitoring** | Collecting known signals and watching for known problems — *"is CPU above 90%? is the error rate too high?"* You decide in advance what to measure and alert on. |
| **Observability** | A property of a system: how well you can **understand its internal state from its external outputs**, including questions you didn't anticipate. Monitoring is something you *do*; observability is something a system *has*. |

### Metrics and alerting

A **metric** is a numeric measurement over time — request rate, latency, memory usage, error count. Metrics are stored as **time series** (a value plus a timestamp, repeated). **Alerting** evaluates rules against those metrics (e.g. *"error rate > 5% for 5 minutes"*) and notifies a human or system when a rule fires.

### Black-box vs white-box monitoring

| Approach | Viewpoint | Example |
|---|---|---|
| **Black-box** | Tests the system **from the outside**, as a user would, with no knowledge of internals | An external probe hits `/health` and checks for HTTP 200 |
| **White-box** | Uses signals **exposed by the system itself** — its internal counters and gauges | The app exposes `http_requests_total` and `queue_depth` that you scrape |

Black-box answers *"is it up for users?"*; white-box answers *"why, and what's happening inside?"* Mature setups use both.

### The common stack

| Tool | Purpose |
|---|---|
| **Prometheus** | Collects and stores time-series **metrics** (pull/scrape model) |
| **Grafana** | Builds **dashboards** and visualizations from metrics |
| **Alertmanager** | Routes, groups and silences **alerts** from Prometheus |
| **Datadog** | Hosted, all-in-one monitoring SaaS (metrics, logs, traces) |
| **Zabbix** | Long-established open-source infrastructure monitoring |

### A typical flow

```
  app exposes metrics  →  Prometheus scrapes & stores them
        →  Grafana dashboards visualize  →  Alertmanager notifies on rule breach
```

> Good monitoring turns "the site feels slow" into "p99 latency on the checkout service jumped at 14:02" — a fact you can act on.
